main_dirt.bias_notes: Historically a 'Golden Rail' track. The dirt surface is hard and fast, rewarding horses that can secure the lead or sit prominently on the rail.
main_dirt.post_bias: Posts 1-4 are consistently advantageous in sprints.

handicapping_angles.speed_bias: Aggressively favor front-runners and pressers. Deep closers are frequently neutralized.
handicapping_angles.one_turn_mile: The one-turn mile at Parx is a speed-dominant event. Inside speed is extremely dangerous here.
handicapping_angles.mid-atlantic_shippers: Be wary of horses shipping in from Laurel or Penn National; Parx often plays faster than those tracks.
